data.title: Amazon says it will fight Italian fine, highlights role of 20,000 SMBs in Italy
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) said on Wednesday it will appeal an Italian court ruling that reduced a fine imposed by the country’s antitrust regulator, arguing that it should not have been fined at all.

The company reiterated that it strongly disagrees with the Italian Competition Authority’s decision and will continue to press its case through the legal process.

“More than half of all annual sales on Amazon in Italy come from SMBs. We have 20,000 Italian SMBs that sell on Amazon, including sellers that manage shipment themselves, and we constantly invest to support their growth,” the company said in a statement to Seeking Alpha.

On Monday, Italy's antitrust authority said it had reduced to 752.4 million euros ($876.3 million) a 1.13-billion-euro fine it imposed on Amazon (AMZN)  in 2021 for abusing its dominant position, restricting competition in e-commerce logistics services in Italy.

The reduction followed a regional administrative court ruling last September.

The group's decision to pursue legal action was first reported by Italian financial newspaper MF.

Updates story to add company statement.

data.title: North Shore Uranium upsizes private placement to $3.23M
data.description:
• North Shore Uranium (NSU:CA) increased its non-brokered private placement to gross proceeds of up to $3.23M, from the previously announced $3M.
  • The offering is priced at $0.25/unit, with each unit comprising one common share and one-half of one share purchase warrant.
  • Each whole warrant allows the purchase of one common share at $0.40 for a period of two years from closing.
  • Net proceeds will fund exploration at the Rio Puerco uranium project in New Mexico, ongoing Saskatchewan uranium exploration, offering costs, and general working capital.

data.title: Glucose Health reports debt-free balance sheet after note conversion
data.description:
• Glucose Health (GLUC) on Wednesday said it has eliminated its debt after converting a $225,000 loan from board member Edmund J. Burke into equity.
  • The company said the loan principal was converted into 1.8 million shares of restricted common stock, while about $30,000 in accrued interest was waived. Burke also returned warrants that would have allowed him to purchase up to 2.25 million additional shares, removing potential future dilution.
  • Glucose Health said the agreement leaves the company with a debt-free balance sheet and follows a similar conversion last month in which preferred shares and accrued dividends were exchanged for common stock.
  • Separately, the company said it is developing a new soluble fiber beverage brand to complement its existing GlucoDown and Fiber Up products, with further details to be announced in a future release.
  • GLUC closed -4.52% at $0.3399.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nexus Uranium announces U.S. symbol change
data.description:
• Nexus Uranium (NEXUF) said on Wednesday that its common shares will commence trading under its new OTC ticker symbol "NEXUF", effective January 14th, 2026.
  • The change from the previous symbol "GIDMF" to "NEXUF" is part of the Company's ongoing efforts to align its U.S. market identity.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Surge Battery Metals to raise $15M in non-brokered LIFE offering
data.description:
• Surge Battery Metals (NILI:CA) (TSXV: NILI) (OTCQX: NILIF) on Wednesday said that it will raise about $15 million through a non-brokered private placement under Canada’s listed issuer financing exemption.
  • The company will issue 16.67 million units at $0.90 each, with each unit comprising one common share and half of a share purchase warrant. Each full warrant will be exercisable at $1.35 per share, starting 60 days after issuance and expiring three years from issuance.
  • Proceeds will be used for costs related to the preliminary and definitive feasibility studies for its Nevada North Lithium Project and for general working capital.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 3, 2026, subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval.
  • NILIF closed -1.91% at $0.6808.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AirJoule Technologies announces pricing of $20M public offering of Class A common stock
data.description:
• AirJoule Technologies (AIRJ) on Wednesday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 6.15M shares of its Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share, at a public offering price of $3.25 per share.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds it receives from the offering to fund growth capital, working capital, and for general corporate purposes.
  • In addition, the company has granted the underwriter a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 923,077 shares of Class A common stock at the public offering price, less the underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 15, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: United Community Banks Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71 in-line, revenue of $278.4M beats by $4.8M
data.description:
• United Community Banks press release (UCB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71 in-line.
  • Revenue of $278.4M (+16.3% Y/Y) beats by $4.8M.
  • United’s return on assets was 1.21%, or 1.22% on an operating basis, up from 1.06% and 1.08%, respectively for the fourth quarter of 2024. Return on common equity was 9.5% and return on tangible common equity on an operating basis was 13.3%. On a pre-tax, pre-provision basis, operating return on assets was 1.78% for the quarter. At year-end, tangible common equity to tangible assets was 9.92%, up 21 basis points from the third quarter.
  • Provision for credit losses was $13.7 million, up $2.3 million from a year ago and up $5.8 million from the third quarter; allowance for credit losses coverage down slightly to 1.16% of total loans; net charge-offs were $16.4 million, or 0.34% of average loans, annualized
  •

data.title: Infosys Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.01, revenue of $5.09B beats by $70M
data.description:
• Infosys press release (INFY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $5.09B (+3.0% Y/Y) beats by $70M.

data.title: The 20 stocks most shorted by hedge funds
data.description:

Here is their list of the S&P 500 (SPY) (IVV) (VOO) stocks most shorted by hedge funds:

data.title: Wingstop reinstates COO role
data.description:
• Wingstop (WING) reinstated the role of chief operating officer with the appointment of Rajneesh Kapoor, previously president of international.
  • Kapoor will oversee the company’s domestic and international franchise development and operations, as well as the company-owned and operated restaurants.
